Bash(cd /Users/prashobh/.openclaw/workspace/doorman && psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
SELECT 
  r.id,
  pt.name AS parent_type, 
  ct.name AS child_type,
  r.relationship_name,
  r.cardinality,
  r.parent_level
FROM object_relationships r
JOIN object_types pt ON pt.id = r.parent_object_type_id
JOIN object_types ct ON ct.id = r.child_object_type_id
ORDER BY r.parent_level, pt.name, ct.name;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
SELECT ot.name, COUNT\(oa.id\) as attr_count
FROM object_types ot
LEFT JOIN object_attributes oa ON oa.object_type_id = ot.id
GROUP BY ot.id, ot.name
ORDER BY ot.name;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Full relationship graph with depths
WITH RECURSIVE rel_tree AS \(
  -- Start from Door \(the primary object type\)
  SELECT 
    ot.id, ot.name, 
    0 AS depth,
    ARRAY[ot.id] AS visited
  FROM object_types ot WHERE ot.name = 'Door'
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    child.id, child.name, 
    rt.depth + 1,
    rt.visited || child.id
  FROM rel_tree rt
  JOIN object_relationships r ON r.parent_object_type_id = rt.id
  JOIN object_types child ON child.id = r.child_object_type_id
  WHERE child.id != ALL\(rt.visited\)
    AND rt.depth < 5
\)
SELECT depth, name, id FROM rel_tree ORDER BY depth, name;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Check samrum_module_object_types and what they contain
SELECT 
  sm.id as module_id, sm.name as module_name,
  mot.object_type_id as samrum_ot_id,
  sot.name_singular, sot.name_plural,
  sm.oms_object_type_id
FROM samrum_modules sm
LEFT JOIN samrum_module_object_types mot ON mot.module_id = sm.id
LEFT JOIN samrum_object_types sot ON sot.id = mot.object_type_id
WHERE sm.id = 269
ORDER BY sm.id;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- All samrum_modules with their oms_object_type_id
SELECT id, name, oms_object_type_id FROM samrum_modules WHERE oms_object_type_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT 20;

-- Check the object_relationships more carefully - are there any multi-hop paths?
SELECT 
  r.id,
  pt.name AS parent_type,
  ct.name AS child_type,
  r.relationship_name,
  r.cardinality,
  r.parent_level,
  -- Does child type also have children?
  \(SELECT COUNT\(*\) FROM object_relationships r2 WHERE r2.parent_object_type_id = r.child_object_type_id\) AS child_has_children
FROM object_relationships r
JOIN object_types pt ON pt.id = r.parent_object_type_id
JOIN object_types ct ON ct.id = r.child_object_type_id
ORDER BY r.parent_level;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Check samrum_relationships \(the samrum schema's own relationship table\)
SELECT 
  r.id, r.caption_singular, r.caption_plural,
  ft.name_singular AS from_type, tt.name_singular AS to_type,
  r.min_relations, r.max_relations, r.sort_order
FROM samrum_relationships r
JOIN samrum_object_types ft ON ft.id = r.from_type_id
JOIN samrum_object_types tt ON tt.id = r.to_type_id
ORDER BY r.sort_order
LIMIT 30;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- How many unique types at each level from 'ID tillträdesobjekt' \(id=116\)?
WITH RECURSIVE rel_tree AS \(
  SELECT id, 0 AS depth, ARRAY[id] AS visited
  FROM samrum_object_types WHERE id = 116
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT child.id, rt.depth + 1, rt.visited || child.id
  FROM rel_tree rt
  JOIN samrum_relationships r ON r.from_type_id = rt.id
  JOIN samrum_object_types child ON child.id = r.to_type_id
  WHERE child.id != ALL\(rt.visited\) AND rt.depth < 5
\)
SELECT depth, COUNT\(DISTINCT id\) as unique_types
FROM rel_tree 
GROUP BY depth 
ORDER BY depth;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Check module-specific relationship assignments for module 269
SELECT 
  smr.id, smr.module_id, smr.allow_edit, smr.read_only,
  sr.caption_singular, sr.caption_plural,
  ft.name_singular AS from_type, tt.name_singular AS to_type
FROM samrum_module_relationships smr
JOIN samrum_relationships sr ON sr.id = smr.relationship_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ft ON ft.id = sr.from_type_id
JOIN samrum_object_types tt ON tt.id = sr.to_type_id
WHERE smr.module_id = 269
ORDER BY sr.sort_order
LIMIT 30;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Get the full tree for module 269 using samrum_module_relationships
-- with distinct levels
WITH RECURSIVE module_tree AS \(
  -- Level 0: Primary type for module 269
  SELECT 
    sot.id, sot.name_singular,
    0 AS depth,
    ARRAY[sot.id] AS visited,
    sot.name_singular AS path,
    NULL::int AS via_relationship_id
  FROM samrum_module_object_types mot
  JOIN samrum_object_types sot ON sot.id = mot.object_type_id
  WHERE mot.module_id = 269 AND mot.is_main_object_type = true
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    child_sot.id, child_sot.name_singular,
    mt.depth + 1,
    mt.visited || child_sot.id,
    mt.path || ' → ' || child_sot.name_singular,
    sr.id
  FROM module_tree mt
  JOIN samrum_module_relationships smr ON smr.module_id = 269
  JOIN samrum_relationships sr ON sr.id = smr.relationship_id AND sr.from_type_id = mt.id
  JOIN samrum_object_types child_sot ON child_sot.id = sr.to_type_id
  WHERE child_sot.id != ALL\(mt.visited\)
    AND mt.depth < 4
\)
SELECT depth, id, name_singular, path 
FROM module_tree 
ORDER BY depth, name_singular;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
WITH RECURSIVE module_tree AS \(
  SELECT 
    sot.id, sot.name_singular,
    0 AS depth,
    ARRAY[sot.id] AS visited,
    sot.name_singular::text AS path,
    NULL::int AS via_relationship_id
  FROM samrum_module_object_types mot
  JOIN samrum_object_types sot ON sot.id = mot.object_type_id
  WHERE mot.module_id = 269 AND mot.is_main_object_type = true
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    child_sot.id, child_sot.name_singular,
    mt.depth + 1,
    mt.visited || child_sot.id,
    \(mt.path || ' → ' || child_sot.name_singular\)::text,
    sr.id
  FROM module_tree mt
  JOIN samrum_module_relationships smr ON smr.module_id = 269
  JOIN samrum_relationships sr ON sr.id = smr.relationship_id AND sr.from_type_id = mt.id
  JOIN samrum_object_types child_sot ON child_sot.id = sr.to_type_id
  WHERE child_sot.id != ALL\(mt.visited\) AND mt.depth < 4
\)
SELECT depth, id, name_singular, path 
FROM module_tree ORDER BY depth, name_singular;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- How many modules have oms_object_type_id set?
SELECT COUNT\(*\) as total_modules, 
       COUNT\(oms_object_type_id\) as with_oms_type,
       COUNT\(*\) - COUNT\(oms_object_type_id\) as without_oms_type
FROM samrum_modules;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- What modules have oms_object_type_id? What are they?
SELECT m.id, m.name, m.oms_object_type_id, ot.name as oms_type_name
FROM samrum_modules m
LEFT JOIN object_types ot ON ot.id = m.oms_object_type_id
WHERE m.oms_object_type_id IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY m.id;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- What columns does module 32 \(Tillträdesobjekt arkitekt\) define?
SELECT mr.id, mr.read_only, mr.allow_edit,
       r.caption_singular, r.caption_plural, r.sys_caption,
       r.sort_order, r.allow_in_lists, r.show_in_lists_default, r.is_requirement,
       r.min_relations, r.max_relations,
       ot_from.name_singular as from_type, ot_to.name_singular as to_type,
       dt.name as data_type
FROM samrum_module_relationships mr
JOIN samrum_relationships r ON r.id = mr.relationship_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ot_from ON ot_from.id = r.from_type_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ot_to ON ot_to.id = r.to_type_id
LEFT JOIN samrum_data_types dt ON dt.id = ot_to.data_type_id
WHERE mr.module_id = 32
ORDER BY r.sort_order
LIMIT 40;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Compare module 32 vs module 269 column counts and types
SELECT mr.module_id, m.name,
       COUNT\(*\) as total_cols,
       COUNT\(CASE WHEN dt.name IN \('Text','Datum','Nummer','Ja/Nej'\) THEN 1 END\) as scalar_cols,
       COUNT\(CASE WHEN dt.name NOT IN \('Text','Datum','Nummer','Ja/Nej'\) OR dt.name IS NULL THEN 1 END\) as relation_cols
FROM samrum_module_relationships mr
JOIN samrum_modules m ON m.id = mr.module_id
JOIN samrum_relationships r ON r.id = mr.relationship_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ot_to ON ot_to.id = r.to_type_id
LEFT JOIN samrum_data_types dt ON dt.id = ot_to.data_type_id
WHERE mr.module_id IN \(32, 63, 64, 65, 66, 269, 141, 142\)
GROUP BY mr.module_id, m.name
ORDER BY mr.module_id;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Module 269 specific columns
SELECT mr.id, mr.read_only, mr.allow_edit,
       r.caption_singular, r.sys_caption,
       r.sort_order, r.show_in_lists_default, r.is_requirement,
       ot_from.name_singular as from_type, ot_to.name_singular as to_type,
       dt.name as data_type
FROM samrum_module_relationships mr
JOIN samrum_relationships r ON r.id = mr.relationship_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ot_from ON ot_from.id = r.from_type_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ot_to ON ot_to.id = r.to_type_id
LEFT JOIN samrum_data_types dt ON dt.id = ot_to.data_type_id
WHERE mr.module_id = 269
ORDER BY r.sort_order;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql postgresql://doorman_user:doorman_pass@localhost:5432/doorman_db -c "
-- Sample: check how samrum_relationships data_type distributes across all module_relationships
SELECT dt.name as data_type, dt.is_complex, dt.is_entity, COUNT\(*\) as cnt
FROM samrum_module_relationships mr
JOIN samrum_relationships r ON r.id = mr.relationship_id
JOIN samrum_object_types ot_to ON ot_to.id = r.to_type_id
LEFT JOIN samrum_data_types dt ON dt.id = ot_to.data_type_id
GROUP BY dt.name, dt.is_complex, dt.is_entity
ORDER BY cnt DESC;
" 2>&1)

Bash(psql "postgresql://a123@localhost:5432/doorman_db" -c "
-- Verify module_view_columns content
SELECT m.id, m.name, COUNT\(mvc.id\) as cols, 
       COUNT\(CASE WHEN mvc.show_by_default THEN 1 END\) as visible_cols,
       COUNT\(CASE WHEN mvc.oms_attribute_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END\) as mapped_cols
FROM samrum_modules m
LEFT JOIN module_view_columns mvc ON mvc.module_id = m.id
WHERE m.id IN \(32, 63, 64, 141, 142, 269\)
GROUP BY m.id, m.name
ORDER BY m.id;
" 2>&1)

Bash(python3 -c "
import csv
path = '/Users/prashobh/.openclaw/workspace/doorman/docs/old-app/existing-app/Database Excel/OTM_ProjectModuleObjectType.csv'
with open\(path, encoding='utf-8-sig'\) as f:
    reader = csv.DictReader\(f\)
    rows = list\(reader\)
    print\('Columns:', reader.fieldnames\)
    print\('Total rows:', len\(rows\)\)
    # Show distribution of OTM_IsMainObjectType
    from collections import Counter
    counts = Counter\(r.get\('OTM_IsMainObjectType', 'MISSING'\) for r in rows\)
    print\('OTM_IsMainObjectType distribution:', dict\(counts\)\)
    print\(\)
    print\('First 5 rows:'\)
    for r in rows[:5]:
        print\(r\)
")

Bash(python3 -c "
import csv
path = '/Users/prashobh/.openclaw/workspace/doorman/docs/old-app/existing-app/Database Excel/OTM_ProjectModuleObjectType.csv'
with open\(path, encoding='utf-8-sig'\) as f:
    reader = csv.DictReader\(f, delimiter=';'\)
    rows = list\(reader\)
    print\('Columns:', reader.fieldnames\)
    print\('Total rows:', len\(rows\)\)
    from collections import Counter
    counts = Counter\(r.get\('OTM_IsMainObjectType', 'MISSING'\) for r in rows\)
    print\('OTM_IsMainObjectType distribution:', dict\(counts\)\)
    # Show rows where OTM_IsMainObjectType = 1
    mains = [r for r in rows if r.get\('OTM_IsMainObjectType'\) == '1']
    print\(f'Main object type rows: {len\(mains\)}'\)
    print\('First 5 main rows:'\)
    for r in mains[:5]:
